title: Define build-time epoch floor for GNSS/date-time freshness
description: User observation: firmware/board build-time stamps can provide an epoch floor for GNSS/date-time freshness. Define how firmware build timestamp, board provisioned timestamp, and GNSS time interact in the date/time provider abstraction: reject/prevent time moving before the build/provision epoch, use it for cold-start freshness sanity, and specify simulator/native tests. No hardware validation required for the design/API/test portion; physical GNSS validation remains separate.
notes: Implemented no-hardware design in docs/firmware-time-provider.md. Defines deterministic firmware build epoch floor, optional valid board provision epoch floor, effective floor=max(build, valid provision), invalid/corrupt/future provision metadata handling, recovery from poisoned provision metadata, source classes, provider state boundary vs current HAL/app location-time fields, acceptance policy, GNSS time/position separation, and native_sim-first test plan for project-LICHEN-2067. Review history: initial review found P2 missing provision epoch validity; fixed. Post-fix review found P1 reproducible-build/generated timestamp trap, P1 undefined future-bound semantics, P2 current HAL field overread risk, and P2 missing source-date/future-poison tests; fixed. Final review found P1 no-build-epoch/provision-validation ambiguity and P2 native_sim optional wording; fixed. Final-final reviews by Bernoulli, Hypatia, and Hegel reported no P0/P1/P2 findings. Checks: git diff --check PASS. EC2/Twister not run because this is docs-only design work; the doc specifies native_sim/qemu tests for the future implementation Bead project-LICHEN-2067.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware firmware date/time epoch floor design in docs/firmware-time-provider.md. Defined deterministic build epoch, valid board provision epoch, GNSS freshness handling, provider boundary, and native_sim/qemu test expectations; final-final three-agent review found no P0/P1/P2 findings; git diff --check passed; EC2 not required for docs-only work.
